SEARCH
Module storm.mysql
Package st.orm.spi.mysql

Class MySQLSqlDialect

java.lang.Object
st.orm.core.spi.DefaultSqlDialect
st.orm.spi.mysql.MySQLSqlDialect
All Implemented Interfaces:
SqlDialect
public class MySQLSqlDialect extends DefaultSqlDialect implements SqlDialect

  • Method Details

    • name

      public String name()
      Returns the name of the SQL dialect.
      Specified by:
      name in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      name in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Returns:
      the name of the SQL dialect.
      Since:
      1.2

    • supportsDeleteAlias

      public boolean supportsDeleteAlias()
      Indicates whether the SQL dialect supports delete aliases.

      Delete aliases allow delete statements to use table aliases in joins, making it easier to filter rows based on related data.

      Specified by:
      supportsDeleteAlias in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      supportsDeleteAlias in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Returns:
      true if delete aliases are supported, false otherwise.

    • supportsMultiValueTuples

      public boolean supportsMultiValueTuples()
      Indicates whether the SQL dialect supports multi-value tuples in the IN clause.
      Specified by:
      supportsMultiValueTuples in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      supportsMultiValueTuples in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Returns:
      true if multi-value tuples are supported, false otherwise.
      Since:
      1.2

      public Pattern getValidIdentifierPattern()
      Returns the pattern for valid identifiers.
      Specified by:
      getValidIdentifierPattern in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      getValidIdentifierPattern in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Returns:
      the pattern for valid identifiers.
      Since:
      1.2

    • isKeyword

      public boolean isKeyword(@Nonnull String name)
      Indicates whether the given name is a keyword in this SQL dialect.
      Specified by:
      isKeyword in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      isKeyword in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Parameters:
      name - the name to check.
      Returns:
      true if the name is a keyword, false otherwise.
      Since:
      1.2

    • escape

      public String escape(@Nonnull String name)
      Escapes the given database identifier (e.g., table or column name) according to this SQL dialect.
      Specified by:
      escape in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      escape in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Parameters:
      name - the identifier to escape (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the escaped identifier

    • getIdentifierPattern

      public Pattern getIdentifierPattern()
      Returns the pattern for identifiers.
      Specified by:
      getIdentifierPattern in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      getIdentifierPattern in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Returns:
      the pattern for identifiers.
      Since:
      1.2

    • getQuoteLiteralPattern

      public Pattern getQuoteLiteralPattern()
      Returns the pattern for string literals.
      Specified by:
      getQuoteLiteralPattern in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      getQuoteLiteralPattern in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Returns:
      the pattern for string literals.
      Since:
      1.2

    • multiColumnExpression

      public String multiColumnExpression(@Nonnull Operator operator, @Nonnull List<SequencedMap<String,Object>> values, @Nonnull Function<Object,String> parameterFunction) throws SqlTemplateException
      Builds a multi-column expression using row value constructor syntax.

      MySQL (8.0.19+) supports tuple comparison syntax for all comparison operators, producing compact SQL like (a, b) > (?, ?) instead of the lexicographic expansion used by the default implementation.

      Operators without clear tuple semantics (IS_NULL, IS_NOT_NULL, LIKE, etc.) fall back to the SqlDialect default implementation.

      Specified by:
      multiColumnExpression in interface SqlDialect
      Parameters:
      operator - the comparison operator to apply.
      values - the multi-row values. Each map represents a single row of column-name-to-value mappings.
      parameterFunction - the function responsible for binding the parameters.
      Returns:
      the SQL fragment representing the multi-column expression.
      Throws:
      SqlTemplateException - if the operator is not supported for multi-column expressions.
      Since:
      1.9

    • limit

      public String limit(int limit)
      Returns a string template for the given limit.
      Specified by:
      limit in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      limit in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Parameters:
      limit - the maximum number of records to return.
      Returns:
      a string template for the given limit.
      Since:
      1.2

    • offset

      public String offset(int offset)
      Returns a string template for the given offset.
      Specified by:
      offset in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      offset in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Parameters:
      offset - the offset.
      Returns:
      a string template for the given offset.
      Since:
      1.2

    • limit

      public String limit(int offset, int limit)
      Returns a string template for the given limit and offset.
      Specified by:
      limit in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      limit in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Parameters:
      offset - the offset.
      limit - the maximum number of records to return.
      Returns:
      a string template for the given limit and offset.
      Since:
      1.2

    • sequenceDiscoveryStrategy

      public SqlDialect.SequenceDiscoveryStrategy sequenceDiscoveryStrategy()
      Returns the strategy for discovering sequences in the database schema.

      MySQL does not support sequences.

      Specified by:
      sequenceDiscoveryStrategy in interface SqlDialect
      Returns:
      SqlDialect.SequenceDiscoveryStrategy.NONE.
      Since:
      1.9

    • useCatalogAsSchema

      public boolean useCatalogAsSchema()
      Returns whether the database uses JDBC catalogs in place of schemas.

      MySQL does not support JDBC schemas. The database name is exposed as the JDBC catalog.

      Specified by:
      useCatalogAsSchema in interface SqlDialect
      Returns:
      true.
      Since:
      1.9

    • constraintDiscoveryStrategy

      public SqlDialect.ConstraintDiscoveryStrategy constraintDiscoveryStrategy()
      Returns the strategy for discovering constraints in the database schema.

      MySQL exposes REFERENCED_TABLE_NAME and REFERENCED_COLUMN_NAME columns in INFORMATION_SCHEMA.KEY_COLUMN_USAGE, which enables efficient bulk foreign key discovery.

      Specified by:
      constraintDiscoveryStrategy in interface SqlDialect
      Returns:
      SqlDialect.ConstraintDiscoveryStrategy.INFORMATION_SCHEMA_REFERENCING.
      Since:
      1.9

    • sequenceNextVal

      public String sequenceNextVal(String sequenceName)
      Returns the SQL statement for getting the next value of the given sequence.
      Specified by:
      sequenceNextVal in interface SqlDialect
      Overrides:
      sequenceNextVal in class DefaultSqlDialect
      Parameters:
      sequenceName - the name of the sequence.
      Returns:
      the SQL statement for getting the next value of the given sequence.
      Since:
      1.6
